methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Joby Aviation is a publicly traded player in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised over $2 billion to date and currently valued above $10 billion. The comparable universe consists of six private firms operating in the same sector, including Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, Aurora Innovation, and ideaForge. No direct public peers exist in this narrow category, limiting traditional trading multiples analysis.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only three same-sector comparables total, and filtering by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold for reliable median analysis. An all-sector median approach would introduce unacceptable noise given the specialized nature of autonomous vehicle development and capital intensity across sub-segments.

The absence of applicable triangulation methods leaves this valuation unsupported by quantitative peer benchmarking. Any range assessment would require either: (i) expansion to adjacent robotics or aerospace peers with acknowledged methodological caveats, (ii) DCF modeling using company-specific cash flow assumptions, or (iii) acquisition precedent analysis in the autonomous systems space. The current $10 billion+ valuation cannot be validated or challenged using the available data. Key uncertainties include the scarcity of matured comparables at Joby's stage and the heterogeneity of business models and timelines across autonomous vehicle platforms.
